title: On the Mountain in Galilee
subtitle: The risen Lord appears to the eleven, and to more than five hundred brethren at once

stillness: He has gone before you into Galilee.
Leave the low places of the valley,
and let your desire ascend the mountain.
gospel: Then the eleven disciples went into Galilee,
to the mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
And seeing him, they adored him;
And Jesus, drawing near, spoke to them, saying:
All power is given to me in heaven and in earth.
Going therefore, teach ye all nations,
baptizing them in the name of the Father,
and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost,
teaching them to observe all things
And behold, I am with you all days,
even to the consummation of the world.
After that, he was seen by more than five hundred brethren at once.
behold: Attend to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the eleven, obedient to the Master's word,
climbing the mountain he had appointed.
It was on a mountain that he showed himself,
for he was to be seen by those who merit
to behold him in the height of heaven,
who, ascending now to the loftiness of perfection,
Behold the Lord standing among them, living, immortal,
and they fall down and adore him with the adoration of latria.
And see: some among them still doubt,
for the newness of the thing overwhelms them.
Behold him draw near, that no doubt may remain in their hearts,
and hear him say: All power is given to me.
consider: Consider that their very doubt became the confirmation of our faith.
For the more they doubted, the more anxiously they inquired;
and the more truly they proved, the more firmly they believed.
Thus they not only strengthened our faith,
but also destroyed the madness of the heretics.
The kind and gentle Lord comforts the believing in their faith,
and strengthens the faith of those who already believe;
he leads back the doubting, and shuts out their doubt,
making known to them to what glory he has come.
He says: All power is given to me in heaven and in earth.
He speaks here from the part of his humanity,
in which he was made a little lower than the angels,
not from the part of his divinity,
in which he is the Father's equal and co-eternal.
For according to the Godhead he is ever almighty over all things;
but according to the manhood he received power over all,
and all things are delivered into his hand,
that the man who was newly made might rule even on earth.
And this power, given to the sinner, he does not despise,
but gathers all without respect of persons:
Go therefore through the whole world; teach all nations.
Going — against the negligent, who will not go;
teach — against the ignorant, who know not how to teach;
all nations — against the accepters of persons,
who will go only to some.
And behold, I am with you all days,
even to the end of the world.
prayer: O Lord Jesus Christ, to whom is given all power in heaven and in earth,
exalted in your humanity, set at the right hand of the Father,
and given the name which is above every name,
that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow,
of things in heaven, on earth, and under the earth:
grant me, a wretched sinner, that ascending the mountain with your disciples,
I may tread underfoot all my earthly desires,
and from the lowest pleasures pass over to the things on high.
And though I doubt, draw near to me as you drew near to them,
and shut out the doubting of my heart;
that adoring you living and immortal,
I may know to what glory you have come,
and be with you all my days,
even to the consummation of the world. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Dubitatio quidem discipulorum, nostræ fidei fuit confirmatio: quippe quo amplius dubitaverunt, eo sollicitius inquisierunt, et quo verius probaverunt, eo firmius crediderunt.
latinJewel.english: The doubt of the disciples was indeed the confirmation of our faith: for the more they doubted, the more anxiously they inquired; and the more truly they proved, the more firmly they believed.
